options.a: a lack of red blood cells in the blood or decreased ability of the haemoglobin to transport oxygen.
options.b: an increase in the number of red blood cells in the blood, decreasing the ability of the haemoglobin to transport oxygen.
options.c: increasing oxygen partial pressure used for the exchange of gases.
options.d: a lack of white blood cells in the blood, decreasing the ability of the haemoglobin to transport carbon dioxide.
